feature_spec.title: HATE specification manual black-box gate
feature_spec.summary: Gate review for accepting docs/process/SPECIFICATION.md as the QEG + workflow-cookbook style specification for harness-auto-test-evidence.
acceptance_criteria: SPECIFICATION.md covers QEG export, workflow-cookbook artifacts, Shipyard-cp advisory evidence, DQ, AETE, fixture, schema, and acceptance
QEG / workflow-cookbook / Shipyard-cp responsibilities are not reimplemented by HATE
Manual black-box gate produces a Go/No-Go decision
business_rules: HATE is an optional evidence producer / normalizer, not the release Gate authority
Shipyard-cp publish approval is not overridden by HATE
Coverage alone does not decide release readiness

assumptions.text: This gate evaluates specification readiness, not runtime implementation readiness.
assumptions.impact_on_coverage: Schema and fixture execution remain follow-up implementation tasks.

observation_set.title: README links to SPECIFICATION.md
observation_set.rationale: The specification must be discoverable from the repository entrypoint.

observation_set.title: QEG boundary is explicit
observation_set.rationale: HATE must not reimplement QEG Gate policy, waiver, approval, retention, or schema migration.

observation_set.title: Shipyard-cp integration is advisory
observation_set.rationale: HATE evidence must not override Shipyard-cp publish approval.

risk_register.scenario: DQ, soft_gap, risk debt, and manual-bb bridge are confused during implementation.
risk_register.rationale: The specification separates the contracts, but fixtures are still follow-up work.

risk_register.scenario: Schema and fixture bodies are not yet implemented.
risk_register.rationale: Acceptable for a specification gate, but not sufficient for runtime readiness.

manual_case_set.title: README links to the specification
steps: Inspect README references and workflow order
expected_results: SPECIFICATION.md is linked and appears in the workflow order

manual_case_set.title: Shipyard-cp evidence remains advisory
expected_results: The section states HATE does not replace Shipyard-cp state machine or publish approval

reasons: SPECIFICATION.md covers QEG export, workflow-cookbook, Shipyard-cp, DQ, AETE, schema, fixture, and acceptance contracts.
P1/P2 manual document review cases pass for specification readiness.
